stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Anne Hutchinson was one of the early settlers who joined the founding of Rhode Island.
  • The Portsmouth Compact was a legal agreement among settlers who left the Massachusetts Bay Colony.
  • It was signed in 1638 to establish a self-governing community on Aquidneck Island.
  • It states that the signers wanted to form a “civil body politic” among themselves and submit to the laws they collectively chose.
  • Hutchinson became a leading figure in the new settlement’s civic life.
stem: The student wants to specify the reason the Portsmouth Compact was created. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: The Portsmouth Compact was signed in 1638 to establish a self-governing community on Aquidneck Island.
Anne Hutchinson, one of the signers of the Portsmouth Compact, became a leading figure in the new settlement’s civic life.
The Portsmouth Compact was a legal agreement among settlers who left the Massachusetts Bay Colony.
Anne Hutchinson was one of the early settlers who joined the founding of Rhode Island.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The sentence specifies the reason the Portsmouth Compact was created: to establish a self-governing community on Aquidneck Island in 1638.

Choice B is incorrect. The sentence describes Hutchinson’s role; it doesn’t specify why the compact was created. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence identifies the compact and its signers but doesn’t specify the reason for its creation. Choice D is incorrect. The sentence describes Hutchinson’s general role in Rhode Island; it doesn’t specify why the Portsmouth Compact was created.
